title: Daily Financial Briefing

keySources.label: 第一财经 — PBOC Jul 15 briefing

marketTone: Jul 15 US indexes closed higher on back-to-back CPI/PPI misses, but SOX fell 2.08% into TSMC's Jul 16 print; Williams flagged tariff-driven inflation while Brent settled $84.95 on Hormuz risk and China channels CNY760bn to private lending.

summary.text: Jul 15 US close (AP): S&P +0.38% to 7,572.40, Nasdaq +0.62% to 26,269.23, Dow +0.29% to 52,658.64 — second session of gains after June CPI/PPI both missed vs consensus.
Jul 15 chip divergence: PHLX Semiconductor Index −2.08% to 12,398.89 (Micron −8.02%) while broad indices rose — profit-taking into TSMC's Jul 16 earnings call.

Fed Williams (Jul 16, 华尔街见闻): tariffs already lifting core goods prices; expects ~+1pp inflation from tariffs through H2 2026 — 'moderately restrictive' stance 'entirely appropriate' despite June CPI/PPI misses.

China policy follow-through: PBOC channeled >CNY760bn (7600亿元) to private-sector relending YTD; Jul 15 buyout RR net +CNY500bn; Q2 GDP 4.3% miss (NBS); 长鑫科技 Changxin DRAM IPO subscription opens Jul 16 (华尔街见闻).

signal: Disinflation is confirmed in June CPI/PPI and ASML re-raised FY26 equipment sales, but Jul 15 SOX −2.08%, Williams' tariff-hawk filter, and Brent $84.95 settle keep the Fed/oil tug alive — TSMC Jul 16 (14:00 Taiwan) is the AI-demand falsifier.
watch: TSMC Q2 results and H2 AI/capex guide (Jul 16, 14:00 Taiwan / 02:00 ET) after ASML's raise, Kospi +6.24%, and SOX profit-taking — does Taiwan's bellwether extend or unwind the semi beta?
globalRegime: The Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%. June CPI and PPI both missed consensus, supporting a Jul hold base case, but NY Fed President Williams (Jul 16) said tariff pass-through is visible in core goods and a moderately restrictive stance remains appropriate. Jul 15 US indexes closed higher (AP) while the PHLX Semi Index fell 2.08% (Yahoo). Brent settled $84.95 and WTI $79.60 (CNBC) as CENTCOM strikes and the Iran port blockade persisted. ASML's second FY26 sales raise and Kospi +6.24% (Jul 15) kept the AI equipment narrative live into TSMC's print.

globalChanged.text: Jul 15 rates/commodities: 10y Treasury 4.55% (Treasury curve, Jul 15); Brent Sep futures settled +$0.22 at $84.95; WTI Aug settled +$0.26 at $79.60 (CNBC).
Jul 15 PHLX Semi Index −2.08% to 12,398.89 — 25 of 30 constituents down; Micron −8.02%, Marvell −7.27%, Intel −4.43% (Yahoo Finance close).
June PPI miss (BLS, Jul 15): final demand −0.3% m/m (vs unchanged consensus), +5.5% y/y; goods less foods and energy +0.2% m/m (vs +0.3%) — second straight disinflation print after Jul 14 CPI miss.

Williams (Jul 16): tariff effects visible in appliances/instruments/luggage/tableware; expects tariffs to add ~1pp to inflation through H2 2026; GDP ~1% and unemployment ~4.5% forecast (华尔街见闻/Fed remarks).
ASML Q2 (Jul 15 IR): net sales €9.326bn, GM 54.0%, net income €2.918bn; FY26 sales outlook raised to €43–45bn; +30% low-NA EUV capacity plan for 2027.
TSMC Q2 earnings conference scheduled Thu Jul 16, 14:00 Taiwan / 02:00 ET; prior Q2 revenue guide US$39.0–40.2bn, GM 65.5%–67.5% (TSMC IR).

globalImplies.text: Back-to-back CPI/PPI misses ease July re-hike urgency, but Williams + Brent $84.95 settle mean September tightening risk is not dead — especially if July energy flows into PCE.
SOX falling while the S&P/Nasdaq rise is classic crowded-AI profit-taking; TSMC guidance decides whether Jul 15's Kospi +6.24% / ASML raise were front-running or sustainable.
CENTCOM strikes and the Iran port blockade keep a supply-risk premium in Brent even on days headline inflation cools.

globalTensions: CPI/PPI say June inflation cooled on energy; Brent $84.95 settle and Williams' tariff warning say forward inflation may re-heat. Broad US indices rose Jul 15 while SOX −2.08% — rates relief did not lift semis uniformly into TSMC.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity and structural relending, cautious on price — Jul 15 PBOC 6-month buyout reverse repo net +CNY500bn; Jul 15 SCIO briefing reaffirmed 7-day OMO as main policy rate and M2 +8% y/y; Jul 16 Caixin reports >CNY760bn private-sector relending YTD; no LPR-cut signal while oil lifts import-inflation risk.

chinaChanged.text: PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR: CNY1.4tn (14000亿元) 6-month operation; CNY900bn maturing → net +CNY500bn (5000亿元) (新华网).
PBOC Jul 15 SCIO briefing (第一财经): Jun M2 CNY356.71tn (+8% y/y); Deputy Governor Zou Lan — guide rates low, 7-day OMO remains main policy rate, structural tools can expand if needed.
Caixin (Jul 16): PBOC has channeled >CNY760bn (7600亿元) via private-sector relending and structural programs YTD toward private-enterprise credit (Jul 15 SCIO briefing).
Q2 GDP 4.3% y/y (NBS, Jul 15) vs ~4.5% consensus — weakest since Q4 2022; Q2 q/q +0.9%; H1 GDP +4.7% to CNY69.57tn; nominal GDP +5.9% y/y ended 12-quarter deflation streak (Caixin).
Jul 15 Asia tape: Kospi +6.24% to 7,284.41 (SK hynix +8.83%); Hang Seng +1.40% to 24,681.10; Shanghai −0.29% to 3,955.58 — A/H split on GDP miss vs global CPI/semi bid (Yonhap/华尔街见闻).
Jul 16: 长鑫科技 Changxin DRAM IPO subscription day at CNY8.66/share (~CNY579bn raise pre-greenshoe) — largest STAR Market IPO to date (华尔街见闻).
BlockBeats (Jul 15): market message Nvidia will resume H20 chip sales in China with US licensing assurance; BTC quoted $116,800 (−3.73% 24h on HTX tape).

chinaImplies.text: CNY500bn buyout RR net plus >CNY760bn structural relending shows quantity easing is active, but Zou Lan's rate guidance and oil near $85 argue against an imminent LPR cut.
Nominal GDP turning positive on AI/oil costs (Caixin) does not fix the real-growth miss — onshore equities still price domestic disappointment while H-shares catch global beta.
Changxin IPO and Nvidia H20 resumption (BlockBeats) are supply-chain positives for China memory/AI hardware, but Jul 15 A-share semis still lagged Korea's CPI/semi rip.

chinaDivergences: Kospi +6.24% and Hang Seng +1.40% vs Shanghai −0.29% after Q2 GDP miss; Jul 15 US SOX −2.08% vs S&P +0.38% — global AI relief is selective, not broad beta. PBOC injecting while A-shares fall = liquidity landed, growth appetite has not.

assetFramework.regime: Fed path via real yields — CPI/PPI-vs-oil/tariff tug; AI earnings as secondary support
assetFramework.driver: Jul 15 close S&P +0.38% / Nasdaq +0.62%; SOX −2.08%; Williams Jul 16 tariff-hawk filter
assetFramework.read: Rates-relief beta held at the index level; semis underperformed into TSMC — AI is a leadership trade, not a blanket lift
assetFramework.invalidator: Equities fall while 10y also falls for 2+ sessions — growth fear replacing the rates regime

assetFramework.regime: Inflation prints vs consensus + Fed communication — data-led range
assetFramework.driver: 10y 4.55% Jul 15 (Treasury); CPI/PPI both missed; Williams Jul 16 kept restrictive stance appropriate
assetFramework.read: Disinflation prints pulled yields lower Jul 14–15; Williams capped how far duration can rally
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y back above 4.65% without a hot data print — term premium / fiscal regime

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with a Hormuz safe-haven bid
assetFramework.driver: July hike odds fell post-CPI/PPI; Brent settled $84.95 Jul 15; Williams flagged tariff inflation
assetFramework.read: Softer Fed path vs oil/geo bid roughly offsets — no decisive DXY break verified at Jul 15 close
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY breaks lower while September hike odds hold elevated with Brent >$85

assetFramework.regime: Real-yield beta day-to-day with a CB/fiscal-hedge floor
assetFramework.driver: Jul 15 COMEX settle −0.42% at $4,044.00; Jul 14 settle $4,061.10 (+1.60%)
assetFramework.read: Gave back part of the CPI bounce as oil stayed firm — classic tug between real yields and energy shock
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold falls >3% while real yields decline — hedge-demand floor questioned

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz blockade premium dominates demand signals
assetFramework.driver: Brent Sep settled $84.95 Jul 15 (+$0.22); WTI Aug $79.60; CENTCOM strikes + Iran port blockade (CNBC)
assetFramework.read: Supply-risk beta intact — overwriting June CPI energy relief for forward inflation
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $78 with Hormuz crossings >40/day

assetFramework.regime: Domestic growth/liquidity vs selective global risk beta — A/H split
assetFramework.driver: Shanghai −0.29% Jul 15 on Q2 GDP 4.3% miss; Hang Seng +1.40%; PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR; Changxin IPO Jul 16
assetFramework.read: Liquidity and structural relending landed but A-shares priced growth disappointment; H-shares caught global CPI/AI bid
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 4,000 on rising volume with DR007 ≤1.40%

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as policy signal; quantity easing without price easing
assetFramework.driver: 7-day rate held 1.40%; buyout RR net +CNY500bn; >CNY760bn private relending YTD; Brent $84.95
assetFramework.read: Stable-fix regime — quantity/structural tools active, LPR pivot not signaled
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→Fed chain
assetFramework.driver: CPI/PPI cooled July hike odds; Brent $84.95; BlockBeats Jul 15 BTC $116,800 (−3.73% 24h HTX)
assetFramework.read: Still priced as liquidity beta — Jul 15 pullback despite CPI relief shows oil/Fed chain dominates crypto headlines
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC rallies while Brent >$85 and September hike odds rise

signals.name: Core disinflation confirmed — CPI and PPI both miss
signals.evidence: CPI core flat m/m / 2.6% y/y; PPI final demand −0.3% m/m / 5.5% y/y (BLS Jul 14–15).
signals.mechanism: Back-to-back downside surprises vs consensus ease near-term re-hike urgency and support duration — unless officials dismiss prints as backward-looking vs oil/tariffs.
signals.disprovedIf: September hike probability reclaims >70% and 10y yields break above 4.65% within one week.

signals.name: SOX profit-taking into TSMC despite CPI relief
signals.evidence: Jul 15 SOX −2.08% to 12,398.89 while S&P +0.38% and Nasdaq +0.62%; Micron −8.02% (Yahoo Finance/AP).
signals.mechanism: Crowded AI/memory beta is vulnerable to a one-day unwind if TSMC's guide disappoints after Kospi +6.24% and ASML's raise.
signals.disprovedIf: TSMC beats with constructive H2 AI commentary and SOX reclaims 12,650 within three sessions.

signals.name: Hormuz oil premium persists at Jul 15 settle
signals.evidence: Brent settled $84.95 Jul 15; CENTCOM strikes and Iran port blockade ongoing (CNBC).
signals.mechanism: Forward energy inflation risk limits how much June CPI/PPI can reprice the Fed path.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent settles below $78 for three sessions with Hormuz crossings >40/day.

signals.name: Williams tariff-hawk filter survives soft inflation prints
signals.evidence: Jul 16 remarks: tariffs adding ~1pp to inflation through H2 2026; core goods prices already rising; restrictive stance appropriate (华尔街见闻).
signals.mechanism: NY Fed leadership sets interpretive frame — markets must weigh backward-looking CPI/PPI against forward tariff/oil pressure.
signals.disprovedIf: Multiple Fed speakers explicitly downplay September hike risk and cite June disinflation as sufficient progress.

signals.name: China quantity easing without A-share beta — A/H split
signals.evidence: PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR Jul 15; >CNY760bn private relending YTD (Caixin); Shanghai −0.29% vs Hang Seng +1.40% Jul 15 (Yonhap/NBS).
signals.mechanism: Liquidity and structural credit land, but Q2 GDP miss keeps onshore growth beta constrained while H-shares track global relief.
signals.disprovedIf: CSI 300 reclaims 4,850 with northbound inflows >CNY5bn for two sessions.

signals.name: TSMC is the falsifier for the ASML/Kospi rebound
signals.evidence: TSMC reports Jul 16 after ASML raise and Kospi +6.24%; prior Q2 guide US$39.0–40.2bn (TSMC IR); SOX already −2.08% Jul 15.
signals.mechanism: If TSMC confirms AI demand/capex, semi beta can extend; a soft guide would mark ASML/Kospi as a crowded relief rally.
signals.disprovedIf: TSMC raises H2 outlook with AI demand commentary and Kospi holds 7,200+ through the print.

watchItems.headline: TSMC Q2 results and H2 AI demand guide
watchItems.why: STRONG ASML signal meets SOX profit-taking and the week's main AI-demand falsifier.
watchItems.watch: Jul 16 14:00 Taiwan — revenue vs US$39.0–40.2bn guide; GM; Q3/FY commentary on AI.
watchItems.trigger: Soft guide or AI demand caution that knocks Kospi >3% and SOX below 12,200.
watchItems.invalidator: In-line/beat with constructive H2 AI commentary and SOX reclaims 12,650 within three sessions.

watchItems.headline: Hormuz / Brent settle band
watchItems.why: STRONG energy-premium signal that can overwrite June CPI/PPI.
watchItems.watch: Brent daily settle (Jul 15: $84.95); CENTCOM / Hormuz flow headlines.
watchItems.trigger: Brent settles above $86 for 3 sessions or crossings stay depressed.
watchItems.invalidator: Brent settle below $80 with crossings >40/day and no new escalation for 5 days.

watchItems.headline: Williams tariff channel vs double inflation miss
watchItems.why: MODERATE Fed-communication signal tests whether CPI/PPI relief sticks.
watchItems.watch: Fed speakers after Williams Jul 16; 10y vs 4.55% Jul 15; core PCE next print.
watchItems.trigger: Two+ Fed officials cite tariff goods inflation to keep September hike odds >60%.
watchItems.invalidator: September hike odds fall below 40% with core PCE ≤0.2% m/m.

watchItems.headline: A/H divergence after GDP miss and PBOC relending
watchItems.why: MODERATE China signal — quantity easing vs growth.
watchItems.watch: Shanghai vs Hang Seng; DR007 vs 1.40%; Caixin/Yicai policy follow-through.
watchItems.trigger: Shanghai closes below 3,900 on rising volume despite Jul 15 net injection.
watchItems.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 4,000 with Hang Seng leadership broadening onshore.

watchItems.headline: Contrarian — CPI/PPI relief fully priced into semis
watchItems.why: Kospi +6.24%, ASML raise, and Jul 15 SOX −2.08% crowd the long-AI trade.
watchItems.watch: Kospi giving back >50% of Jul 15 gain into/after TSMC.
watchItems.trigger: Both Kospi fade and TSMC soft guide = crowded relief trade unwinding.
watchItems.invalidator: Kospi holds gains through TSMC with SOX extending.

sources: Bureau of Labor Statistics (June CPI archive; June PPI), AP (Jul 15 US close), US Treasury yield curve, CNBC (Jul 15 Brent/WTI settle), Yahoo Finance (SOX), ASML IR (Q2 2026), TSMC IR (Q2 2026 page), NBS (H1/Q2 GDP), 新华网 (PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR), Caixin Global (Jul 15 GDP deflator; Jul 16 private relending), 第一财经/Yicai (Jul 15 PBOC SCIO briefing), 华尔街见闻 (Williams Jul 16; Changxin IPO; A/H tape), BlockBeats (Jul 15 BTC/H20), GoldSeek/Dow Jones (COMEX gold Jul 15 settle), Yonhap (Kospi Jul 15).
singleSource: Williams GDP ~1% / unemployment ~4.5% forecasts (华尔街见闻 paraphrase of Jul 16 remarks — not a primary Fed release text); BlockBeats BTC $116,800 (HTX tape, single crypto venue).
